artPrompt: A dramatic sorcery moment as decades of decay collapse all at once: the crumbling treatment plant buckles, rusted pipes bursting and spewing toxic green sludge in great arcs while bioluminescent runoff floods the cratered ground under a violently bruised stormy sky. The cascading failure climbs the wide landscape frame from flooded base to collapsing tanks above. Wide 4:3 landscape, Gruul murky green and rust red, sickly glow, toxic ecological menace, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: The Reservoir Bill Comes Due
text:
Decades of deferred maintenance arrive at once. Each opponent loses life equal to the number of permanents they control minus the number of sludge counters on creatures you control, minimum zero, calculated as both players simultaneously, which doesn't fully make sense, so just do it as fast as you can before anyone objects.
Then you draw cards equal to the total life lost this way, until you're uncomfortable, then one more.
flavorText: The invoice was forty years old and written in three eyes' worth of handwriting.
